methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Jimmy Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Antoine Guyot and Mathilde Grivet, having raised €80M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 nuclear energy peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. The nuclear energy peer set is too sparse at Jimmy Energy's stage: only 1 same-stage comparable and 1 adjacent-stage peer were identified, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum required for statistical reliability. A sector-wide median would conflate heterogeneous development stages and business models, introducing material bias. Other triangulation methods (precedent transactions, DCF-derived implied values) cannot be applied without additional transaction or financial projection data.

This analysis therefore cannot support a specific valuation range at this time. The primary constraint is data insufficiency rather than analytical limitation. To progress valuation, one would need either: transaction multiples from recent nuclear energy financings at comparable stages; granular financial projections for Jimmy Energy or close peers; or a materially expanded comparable set with disclosed valuations. Frontier-stage nuclear companies remain lightly traded and sparsely reported, amplifying the inherent uncertainty in cross-sectional pricing.
